question: Does the NuScale vacuum containment design provide adequate pressure suppression during a LOCA?

nuscale_ch01_0169.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.4 Other Fission Product Control Systems
nuscale_ch01_0169.summary: The NuScale design does not include traditional fission product clean-up or secondary containment systems, as its passive safety features and submerged containment vessel inherently control fission products. Therefore, many aspects of SRP 6.5.3 guidance are not applicable, except for certain containment parameters like leakage rates. The only credited engineered safety feature (ESF) for fission product control in NuScale is the containment vessel with its isolation systems.

nuscale_ch01_0171.title: SRP 6.5.5, Rev 1: Pressure Suppression Pool as a Fission Product Cleanup SystemAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0171.summary: The section explains that SRP 6.5.5, which addresses the use of a pressure suppression pool as a fission product cleanup system, is not applicable to the NuScale reactor design because NuScale does not use or credit a suppression pool, a feature relevant only to large LWRs like BWRs. Therefore, the associated regulatory criteria do not apply to NuScale’s safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0779.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.4 Programmatic Requirements
nuscale_ch01_0779.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCA) as outlined in DSRS 15.6.5 and related SRP sections. It notes full or partial conformance with requirements for calculating radiological consequences and containment leakage, with some criteria superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3 and certain guidance applicable only to BWRs. The table documents how NuScale addresses each regulatory requirement.

nuscale_ch01_0780.title: SRP 15.6.5.A, Rev 1: Radiological Consequences of a Design Basis Loss-of-Coolant Accident Including Containment Leakage ContributionII.1 Calculated Doses and Containment Leakage Contribution
nuscale_ch01_0780.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for evaluating radiological consequences of a design basis loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA), including containment leakage. It notes that while NuScale’s analysis methods generally conform to guidance in SRP 15.6.5.A, some acceptance criteria and analysis models are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3, and certain guidance applies only to boiling water reactors (BWRs). The section also confirms full conformance regarding engineered safety feature (ESF) system leakage assumptions.

nuscale_ch01_0022.title: Engineered Safety Feature Materials
nuscale_ch01_0022.summary: The section describes how the NuScale reactor’s containment vessel (CNV) is maintained under vacuum to reduce corrosion, facilitate leak detection, and minimize heat transfer, which enhances safety and reduces debris. The design relies on passive safety features, such as natural cooling and elimination of large external piping, resulting in improved safety, lower core damage frequency, and a smaller radioactive source term compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate the need for external power and large coolant loops, significantly lowering the risk of core damage and improving safety compared to traditional nuclear plants.

nuscale_ch01_0357.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.1 CVCS Functional Performance during Adverse Environmental Phenomena; Pumping Capacity; and defense-in-depth RCS makeup
nuscale_ch01_0357.summary: This section reviews the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) and related systems for conformance with regulatory criteria. It finds that the CVCS partially meets requirements for functional performance during adverse environmental conditions, with its only safety-related function being the prevention of inadvertent boron dilution in the reactor coolant system. The system fully conforms to single failure criteria and other requirements where applicable, with some criteria not relevant due to design differences from traditional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0701.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.4 Analytical ParametersMaximize Heat Transfer from RCS to Containment and Reactor Pool
nuscale_ch01_0701.summary: This section documents NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.6, specifically regarding analytical parameters for the loss of containment vacuum scenario. It confirms that the analysis maximizes heat transfer from the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) to the containment and reactor pool, and that all relevant parameters conform to regulatory requirements. No exceptions or deviations are noted for this criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0601.title: SRP 13.3, Rev 3: Emergency PlanningII.22 ITAAC Associated with Design Certification Application
nuscale_ch01_0601.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s conformance with emergency planning regulatory criteria from SRP 13.3, Rev 3. Most emergency planning requirements are not applicable to the design certification application and are instead the responsibility of the Combined License (COL) applicant, except for the emergency response data system, which is partially addressed in the NuScale design but requires site-specific implementation by the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0604.title: SRP 13.3, Rev 3: Emergency PlanningII.25 Design and Implementation of Emergency Response Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0604.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report partially conforms to SRP 13.3, Rev 3, Section II.25, as it includes a technical support center in its design, while the operational support center and emergency operations facility are the responsibility of the Combined License (COL) applicant referencing the NuScale design. Thus, full implementation of emergency response facilities depends on actions taken by the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0776.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.1 Evaluation of ECCS Performance
nuscale_ch01_0776.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCAs) and related radiological consequences. It notes that NuScale’s design partially conforms to certain guidance, with some acceptance criteria superseded by more current regulatory sections, and that the design supports exemptions from specific requirements where technically justified. The evaluation confirms that relevant safety analyses and methodologies are appropriately addressed for the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0699.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.2 Analytical ParametersConservative Scram Used
nuscale_ch01_0699.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.6, specifically regarding analytical parameters used in the loss of containment vacuum scenario. It confirms that conservative assumptions, such as using a conservative scram, are applied and that the design meets the relevant regulatory requirements without exceptions for this parameter.

nuscale_ch01_0026.title: Fission Product Removal and Control Systems
nuscale_ch01_0026.summary: The only credited fission product removal and control system in the NuScale design is the containment vessel (CNV) working with the containment isolation system. Fission product control is achieved inherently by the design, as the CNV atmosphere is passively depleted of fission products.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the NuScale Power Plant’s major design features and parameters with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses significantly smaller modules, fewer fuel assemblies, and simplified systems such as the elimination of external coolant loops and pumps. The comparison is intended to illustrate the unique aspects and safety system differences of the NuScale design relative to conventional PWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0694.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.2 Specific CriteriaCladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0694.summary: This section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria regarding radiological consequences of main steam line failures outside containment, specifically referencing SRP 15.1.5.A and DSRS 15.1.6. It notes partial conformance where NuScale applies relevant guidance, but clarifies that certain radiological acceptance criteria and methodologies are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3. Additionally, it confirms conformance with reactor coolant pressure criteria under DSRS 15.1.6.

nuscale_ch01_0781.title: SRP 15.6.5.A, Rev 1: Radiological Consequences of a Design Basis Loss-of-Coolant Accident Including Containment Leakage ContributionII.2 Model for and Calculation of Post-LOCA Containment Leakage Contribution
nuscale_ch01_0781.summary: This section discusses NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for evaluating radiological consequences of a design basis loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA), specifically regarding containment leakage. It notes that while the guidance for calculating post-LOCA containment leakage is applicable, the acceptance criteria and analysis model are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3, and some guidance applies only to boiling water reactors (BWRs). Overall, NuScale partially conforms to the relevant guidance, with certain requirements not applicable or replaced by updated criteria.

nuscale_ch01_0717.title: DSRS 15.2.6, Rev 0: Loss of nullmergency AC Power to the Station AuxiliariesII.2 Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0717.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various accident scenarios, including loss of nonemergency AC power and feedwater system failures. For each criterion, the document states whether NuScale’s analysis and categorization (such as AOO and IE) meet the requirements, with specific notes on fuel cladding integrity and analytical methods. Overall, NuScale’s safety analysis is shown to conform to the relevant DSRS sections, with some additional details provided for specific scenarios.

nuscale_ch01_0643.title: DSRS 15.0, Rev 0: Introduction - Transient and Accident AnalysesI.1 Categorization of Transients and Accidents
nuscale_ch01_0643.summary: This section outlines NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for transient and accident analyses, specifically referencing DSRS 15.0, Rev 0. It confirms that the methodology for inspections, tests, analyses, and acceptance criteria (ITAAC) follows the guidance provided in SRP 14.3, and states that the categorization of transients and accidents conforms to the relevant standards. The table summarizes compliance with NUREG-0800 and the Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS).

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0708.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.D Instrument Setpoints - Impact on Plant Response
nuscale_ch01_0708.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), even though NuScale does not have a steam pressure regulator. For instrument setpoints and their impact on plant response, as well as other safety-related criteria, the design is shown to conform with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0652.title: DSRS 15.0, Rev 0: Introduction - Transient and Accident AnalysesI.8.B Sequence of Events and Systems Operation
nuscale_ch01_0652.summary: This section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for transient and accident analyses, noting areas of full and partial compliance with DSRS 15.0 requirements. Most criteria are met, but some, such as frequency analysis for AOOs and specific acceptance criteria for fuel cladding and LOCA, only partially conform due to methodological differences. The sequence of events and systems operation criterion is also only partially met, with one item specifically applicable to COL applicants.

edges.description: Section A states that the NuScale design does not include or require pressure suppression systems, while Section B clarifies that the regulatory criteria for pressure suppression pools are not applicable to the NuScale design, connecting them through the absence of pressure suppression features in the design and the inapplicability of related regulatory requirements.
